sections.instruction: Questions 1-6: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY. Questions 7-10: Complete the table below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.
Questions 11-15: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 16-20: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.content: Why did a port originally develop at Manham?
options: A It was safe from enemy attack
B It was convenient for river transport
C It had a good position on the sea coast

questions.content: What caused Manham’s sudden expansion during the Industrial Revolution?
options: A the improvement in mining techniques
B the increase in demand for metals
C the discovery of tin in the area

questions.content: Why did rocks have to be sent away from Manham to be processed?
options: C lack of skills among local people

questions.content: What happened when the port declined in the twentieth century?
options: C Buildings were used for new purposes

questions.content: What did the Manham Trust hope to do?
options: A discover the location of the original port
B provide jobs for the unemployed

sections.instruction: Questions 21 and 22: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 23 and 24: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 25-30: What source of information should Tim use at each of the following stages of the work placement? Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to questions 25-30.

questions.content: Which TWO skills did Laura improve as a result of her work placement?
Which TWO immediate benefits did the company get from Laura’s work placement?

sections.instruction: Questions 31-33: Choose the correct letter A, B or C. Questions 34-40: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY.
sections.passage_or_context: Nanotechnology: technology on a small scale

questions.content: The speaker says that one problem with nanotechnology is that
options: A it could threaten our way of life
B it could be used to spy on people
C it is misunderstood by the public

questions.content: According to the speaker, some scientists believe that nano-particles
options: A should be restricted to secure environments
B should be used with more caution
C should only be developed for essential products

questions.content: In the speaker’s opinion, research into nanotechnology
options: A has yet to win popular support
B could be seen as unethical

questions.content:
Uses of Nanotechnology
Transport
• Nanotechnology could allow the development of stronger {{BLANK}}
• Planes would be much lighter in weight.
• {{BLANK}} travel will be made available to the masses.
Technology
• Computers will be even smaller, faster, and will have a greater {{BLANK}}
• {{BLANK}} energy will become more affordable.
The Environment
• Nano-robots could rebuild the ozone layer.
• Pollutants such as {{BLANK}} could be removed from water more easily.
• There will be no {{BLANK}} from manufacturing.
Health and Medicine
• New methods of food production could eradicate famine.
• Analysis of medical {{BLANK}} will be speeded up.
• Life expectancy could be increased.
